observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
A.	Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to finalize the reconciliation of their records in order to establish the proper and accurate amount of RPT and SET Receivables;
B.	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the necessary adjustments as a result of the reconciliation with the Treasurer’s Office and Assessors Office; and
C.	Direct the concerned officials to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 20, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant review and reconcile the difference of P32,688,870.12 noted between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to balance the reciprocal accounts in order to ensure the fair presentation of the Management’s financial statements pursuant to Section 112 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjustments to present a fair and reliable balance of the Property, Plant and Equipment, in accordance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly record future financial assistance to be received, under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and ensure timely compliance or implementation of programs/purpose intended by the grant, pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that the Management require the immediate liquidation of fund transfers and stop granting additional fund transfers of assistance to agencies which still have unliquidated cash advances, properly record fund transfers to various agencies and take necessary actions on the dormant receivable accounts, as previously recommended. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipality consider the guidelines on COA Circular 2023-008 for the disposition of dormant accounts receivable, in order to ensure that the balances of the Due from NGAs, LGUs and NGOs are presented fairly in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
A.	Ensure the timely reclassification of completed projects from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE account immediately upon project completion, as required under Section 50, Chapter 3 of the Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I;
B.	Recognize depreciation expenses in the period the assets become available for use, in accordance with Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17, to reflect accurate balances in the Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance; and
C.	Review and adjust the accounting records for CY 2024 and compute the depreciation expense of the infrastructure projects from the period the PPE accounts were available for use until the end of the year, thereby ensuring the reliability of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:
A. Instruct the General Services Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties by the Disposal Committee, in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024 or the Revised Manual on Disposal of Government Properties and Title V of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to expedite the reconciliation of their records for PPE accounts and adjust the books of accounts and property records once the disposal procedures have been completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant charge repairs and maintenance expenses to properties only owned by the Municipality, in accordance with paragraph 23, IPSAS 17 on the cost of labor, consumables and small parts. We also recommended that the Management submit other approved ordinances, if any, for the realignment/augmentation or supplemental budgets for the excess expenditures incurred on the Repairs and Maintenance account, otherwise, said amount will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to apply the proper valuation and measurement of Biological Assets as prescribed in IPSAS 27, reconcile the amount reported between the Statement of Financial Position and the records of the Municipal Agriculturist and include in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosures as per IPSAS 27; and
Prepare the proper forms for Ledger Card and Property Card prescribed in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

observations.recommendation:

The Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry at the end of the year to record the restoration of cash equivalent to the unreleased checks, and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts;

The Acting Municipal Treasurer to inform the payees to claim their checks as soon as it is available for release to avoid voluminous number of unclaimed checks and to facilitate timely submission of corresponding disbursement vouchers; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Thru the Inventory Committee created, update the property records based on the results of physical inventory, and reconcile the same with accounting records; and
Undertake all the procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management direct the concerned Department Heads to undertake their responsibilities provided in COA Circualr No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 for the preparation and submission of the required reports in order to establish the appropriate and fair balance of the Road Network account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the receipt of an item of inventory as an asset, in accordance with Section 51, Chapter 3, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units,  and record the consumption/ use thereof pursuant to Paragraph 44 of PPSAS 12.
We also recommended that the Management maintain the necessary documents and records as a reference for materials and supplies availability and reconciliation of accounts.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense for the Power Supply System and Other Infrastructure accounts, prepare the lapsing schedule and draw the necessary journal entry to take up depreciation expense for the current year and record adjustment in government equity account for unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expense to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to identify the specific details and nature of the “unreconciled/unverified” amounts and report/adjust the same in order to present fairly the financial statements, in accordance with Section 27 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

We recommended that the Management demand the liquidation of the fund transfers and ask a refund for the unutilized balances, if any, to present fairly the assets and equity account in the financial statement and to ensure that programs and projects are promptly implemented.
We also recommended that the Management exert effort to identify dormant accounts, if any, as defined under  Section 5.6 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and take necessary actions as provided thereon.

We recommended that the Management fast-track the implementation of the current year and continuing development projects, in order to avoid accumulation of unimplemented projects, and to ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund in the ensuing year, pursuant to Section 5 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management submit the required documents to the Auditor, and henceforth, ensure that payments for the SRA are supported with relevant and pertinent documents pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445 to establish compliance with the conditions set under AO No. 36 dated November 16, 2020 and DBM – DOH Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.

We recommended that the Management require concerned offices to adequately and properly plan work assignments/activities or to hire sufficient number of personnel to meet the rigorous work details in the Municipality and strictly comply with  Section 3 of Civil Cervice Commission and Department of Budget and Management Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2015, November 25, 2015 for overtime services rendered. We also recommended that the Management ensure that all disbursements are within the appropriated amount of each purpose, to safeguard government funds against loss or wastage, through illegal or improper disposition.

We recommended that the Management optimize the utilization of its 20% Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of DBM-DOF- DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and avoid the charging of MOOE and other administrative expenditures to this fund in order to deliver greater socio- economic and environmental management impacts for its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review, analyze and reconcile the intra-agency accounts and exert efforts to locate the documents supporting the transfers to facilitate reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments in the financial records of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management strictly require the officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purposes for which the same were granted have been served pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1145, and for local travels, within 30 days as prescribed in Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to facilitate the timely recording of the expenditures in the proper expense and related accounts.

We recommended Management to submit Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) of the subject transfers to the Audit Team for verification and issue demand letters to the recipients/borrowers to settle their obligations and/or liquidate immediately the fund transfers or if the fund remained unutilized, demand its full refund to the LGU. If despite series of demands, the transfers remained unliquidated or uncollected, comply with the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in requesting for write-off of dormant receivable accounts.

We recommended that management require the contractors to submit warranty security posted in favor of the Municipality to guaranty recovery in case of structural defects and failures attributable to improper construction, use of inferior quality/substandard materials, and any violation of the contract plans and specifications that may arise from the final acceptance up to the period prescribed by Section 62.2.3.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184. We also recommend that Certificate of final acceptance should only be issued after the defects liability period.

We recommended that the LGU Management carefully and judiciously plan and implement the utilization of its 20% Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 and avoid the charging of MOOE and other administrative expenditures, thereby deliver greater socio- economic and environmental management impacts for its constituents.

We recommended that Management specifically identify the projects in the Annual Investment Plan consistent with the allowable projects provided in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and ensure that the expenditures programmed partake the nature of investment and capital expenditure thru the preparation of the corresponding Programs of Works to vouch proper charging of fuel, oil and lubricants expenditures to the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that management include projects/activities/programs chargeable against the “Trust Liabilities- DRRMF” account in the annual LDRRMFIP of the current year pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and the same be prepared in substantial adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby make this trust fund available for disbursement.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) refrain from inclusion of LDRRM plans that do not identify specific projects, programs and activities but rather identify those that address disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery issues and concerns pursuant to NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month to comply with the provisions of the IRR of RA 10121 and Sec. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 thereby facilitate prompt audit and immediate communication of observation relative to the compliance of the Municipality on the proper utilization, accounting and reporting requirements therefor, towards a more effective comprehensive mitigation and response to disasters affecting the locality.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Local School Board of the Municipality to submit an explanation or justification why the amount of P268,368.07 may be properly charged against the Special Education Fund (SEF). Henceforth, we recommend Management to ensure the judicious and prudent use of the SEF in accordance with DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 S. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Apply the limitations imposed in CSC DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015 to overtime services rendered by personnel under Job Order status to ensure that the Municipality receive the most efficient and effective services from said personnel and to avoid irregular disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012; and
Review its workforce component to effectively allocate resources of the LGU that will make its rescue activity more efficient and effective.

We recommend that the Management properly accomplish the Purchase Requests in accordance with the instructions provided by the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units Volume 2 and indicate the estimated cost of the items subject for purchase as stated in the PR, thereby obtain just and advantageous price quotations in the procurement activities of the Municipality within the limits of the budget estimates in the exercise of economy and efficiency.

We recommended that Management:
Submit within five (5) days the contracts and supporting procurement documents of the above-mentioned projects as well as those projects implemented but were not included in the quarterly report on the programs, projects and activities in adherence to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
Comply with the mandatory submission of all contracts and purchase orders including all the supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation thereof and advice Management promptly of any noted deficiencies and defects in the contracts entered into by the Municipality.

We recommend Management to submit the identified lacking documents and ensure that every contract awarded through public bidding shall be duly supported with the necessary documents to evaluate whether the procurement was compliant with the relevant provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, otherwise the issuance of Notice of Suspension may be warranted.

We recommended that management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines on the grant of communication allowances to officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economical reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the immediate settlement of the final and executory audit disallowance amounting to P420,000.00 in accordance with the provisions of the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit, otherwise, this Office shall undertake the next prescribed action pursuant to said rule.

We recommended that the LGU recognize the acquired drugs and medicines as assets whether through purchase or donation and to recognize expense only upon the actual distribution of the goods. In addition, Management shall maintain record of its inventory for drugs and medicines and include the same in the conduct of physical count. Furthermore, the accounting department shall make necessary adjustment to correct the misstatement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management the following:

a)	Ensure that at least five percent (5%) of the total agency budget appropriations authorized under its annual GAA correspond to activities supporting GAD plans and programs.

b)	Include in the mandated appropriation for GAD only those projects that have a well-defined gender issue to be addressed.

c)	Include in its Plan and Budget those GAD concerns cited under PPGD for 1995-2025 and other relevant laws on GAD.

d)	Make reference with Annex A of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01 in the formulation of its GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office submit to the Auditor all financial transactions and accounts of the Municipality within the prescribed period and for the Treasury Office to transmit to the Accounting Office within the most reasonable time all documents and reports to ensure that the latter could comply with regulation.

We recommended that the Management require the contractor to provide the warranty security for the project and ensure that warranty security/s be secured from the contractor/s upon final acceptance of projects.

We recommended that the LGU-Management shall submit copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents to facilitate systematic and effective review process and for the timely communication of defects and/or deficiencies found, if any, to avoid suspensions in audit.

We recommended that the management submit the required monthly monitoring report on Government Infrastructure Projects as provided by COA Circular No. 2013-004 to facilitate early review and evaluation of all on-going infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management deduct portion of the advance payment together with the retention money in accordance with the provisions of Annex  “E” of the above mentioned regulation to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended management to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) with the actual date of completion of the project stated in the document to determine whether the project was completed on/ or before the target date or it was completed beyond the target completion date in which case the Liquidated Damages (LD) shall be applied equal to 1/10 of 1% of the unperformed portion of work per every day of delay pursuant to Section 8 of “Annex E” of Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that management fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize its utilization and the timely delivery of desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that the annual LDRRMFIP be formulated in substantial adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend management the following:
Ensure that at least five percent (5%) of the total agency budget appropriations authorized under its annual GAA correspond to activities supporting GAD plans and programs.
Include in the mandated appropriation for GAD only those projects that have a well-defined gender issue to be addressed.
Include in its Plan and Budget those GAD concerns cited under PPGD for 1995-2025 and other relevant laws on GAD.
Make reference with Annex A of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01 in the formulation of its GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that management include all procurement activity that it plans to undertake within the calendar year in the APP and ensure that all pertinent information/data as provided under Sec. 3.2 GPPB Circular 1-2009 and Sec. 7.3 of the IRR of RA 9184 are included therein.
We recommended that management stop the payment of the above mentioned expenditure to achieve the principle of separation of the church and the state.

We recommended that concerned officials strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 par (e) & (g) of GAAM Volume I on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We recommended management to undertake the following measures:

1. Strictly enforce the use of the Driver's Trip Tickets before authorizing the use of vehicles;

2. Require the Municipal Accountant to use a Pre-Numbered Trip Ticket to avoid multiple usage of Control Numbers.

3.	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption with complete documentations in compliance to Section 361 of GAAM Volume I, and COA Circular No. 75-6 to prevent the wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditure for fuel consumption of government vehicles.

4. Refrain from providing fuel to privately-owned vehicles.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned key officials utilize the 20% development fund solely for its intended purpose. Follow strictly the provision of DILG-DBM Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the granting of cash advances to Officials and employees of the LGU with unliquidated cash advance.
Ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
Follow strictly with the existing regulations on the granting of cash advances.
Instruct the Accountant to comply strictly with the rules and regulations on cash advance

	Instruct the Accountant to comply strictly with the rules and regulations on cash advance

We recommended that the Municipality avail of public bidding as a primary mode of procurement to obtain the most advantageous price for the government. Alternative mode of procurement can be availed of only in exceptional cases and such exceptions must be resorted to in a lawful manner.

We recommended that the municipality stop the payment of Cash Gift to personnel of other government agency on detail with the LGU and to fully adhere with pertinent provisions of the above cited circulars. Require the concerned official to refund the amount received as cash gift.

We recommended that management submit copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents to facilitate systematic and effective review process and the timely communication of defects and/or deficiencies found, if any, to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
